title: Per-state election event inventory
description: The frontend-readable catalogue of which election events exist for each state. The backend's events.py registry is the source of truth at backend-build time; this JSON is the static-app view, hand-authored and kept consistent via CI test (backend/tests/test_election_events_consistency.py). Per-state arrays let route components resolve `defaultEventForState(stateCode)` without bundling Python. National events (e.g. LsGenApr2024) appear in every relevant state's array as a 'slice' — same event_id, same artifact directory, the per-state read just selects the right row. Per ADR-0023.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Per-state event inventory keyed by ECI state code. Each event entry: event_id, kind (assembly/lok_sabha/by_election), display, polled_on, optional default flag (at most one default per state — defines what /s/<state>/elections resolves to).

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12 / ADR-0002. Empty array signals hand-authored content (the canonical case here — this file mirrors backend/yen_gov/sources/eci/events.py, validated by CI consistency test).

states.description: Map from ECI state code (e.g. S22, U07) to a chronological list (most recent first) of election events for which our datasets/elections/ tree contains parsed data. States with NO election data on disk are omitted entirely; the loader returns an empty array for them, which the UI renders as 'no election data ingested yet'.

event_id.description: ECI event identifier matching the on-disk grouping under datasets/elections/<event_id>/<state_code>/. Mirrors the value emitted by events.py event_id_for(state, year).

kind.description: Political body the event elected. 'assembly' is the state Vidhan Sabha (citizen's primary state government); 'lok_sabha' is the national Lok Sabha (the state's MPs sent to Delhi — kept separate per the cause/consequence doctrine in ADR-0023, never co-mingled with assembly results); 'by_election' is a partial mid-term.

display.description: Citizen-facing label. Cohort codes (e.g. AcGenJun2024) MUST NOT appear in this string — citizens think in (state, year), not ECI cohort artifact. Format guideline: '<State> Assembly · <Month YYYY>' or '<State> · Lok Sabha <YYYY>'.

polled_on.description: Polling date (or last polling date for multi-phase events). Drives the recency rule in StateOverview (election leads <90d post-poll, otherwise government-card leads). For multi-state cohorts polling on different days, this is the state-specific date.

term_end_estimated.description: Estimated term-end (typically polled_on + 5 years per Article 172). Null for Lok Sabha slices and by-elections. Used to surface 'next election due' chrome in the final year of term.

default.description: At most one event per state may set default: true. Defines what /s/<state>/elections (no event_id segment) resolves to, and which event the StateOverview's 'last election' card pulls from. Convention: the most recent assembly election.

data_status.description: Tells the UI what to expect on disk. 'complete' = full result.summary.json + per-AC results emitted (the ingested case); 'partial' = some artifacts present (rare); 'pending_upstream' = ECI has not yet published (e.g. Bihar 2025), UI surfaces an honest 'awaiting publication' message rather than a 404.

notes.description: Optional context for citizen-facing display (e.g. 'Held concurrently with Lok Sabha 2024').
